metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_202
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Flot1 is required for PKC-triggered internalization and Amphiphysin-induced reverse transport of DA in primary DA neurons. It is essential for membrane microdomain localization of dopamine transporter.

20/2/2017 Pim
-primary paper: Flot1 is required for PKC-triggered internalization and Amphiphysin-induced reverse transport of DA in primary DA neuron. This role at the level of the synapse is not shown.
-SynGO ID #201: Flot1 is localized in synapses of hippocamal neurons. Pre or postsynaptic localization cannot be distinguished based on the presented data.
-Inference: Flot1 regulates "NT reuptake" in the synapse.

8/8/2017 Pim
- On second thought I have agreed for the term 'neurotransmitter reuptake (GO:0098810). Flot1 is shown to mediate PKC-triggered internalization and Amphiphysin-induced reverse transport of DA in primary DA neurons. According to SynGO guidelines this does not have to happen AT the synapse; BPs are included when relevant for synaptic function. Without evidence shown this is implied by the authors "Plasmalemmal neurotransmitter transporters (NTTs) regulate the level of neurotransmitters, such as dopamine (DA) and glutamate, after their release at brain synapses". Another point of doubt is the 're-' part of the term 'reuptake'. No experiments are shown in a synaptic context and secretion from synapses and consecutive re-uptake are not shown in the paper. I have allowed this under an IC evidence type: the justification here is that whatever Dopamine is around cells that this has to be secreted by a synapse at some time.. Experimental description: HeLa and HEK293 cells.
Flotillin-1 constructs. hFlot1 was purchased from Origene. Midbrain DA neurons were cultured from mice expressing RFP driven by the tyrosine hydroxylase promoter.
